text.title: multi-agent-design-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for multi-agent-design-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Assuming previous stage output will be perfectly formatted.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Real LLM outputs are messy. Add validation and error recovery at each stage.

failure-mode.description: Weak synthesizer loses value from good parallel work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parallel quality only matters if synthesis is strong. Use an Opus-grade synthesizer.

failure-mode.description: Generator and critic from the same base model converge on shared blind spots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs trained on the same data share biases. Use different models or fine-tuned variants.

failure-mode.description: Loop exits at max_iterations regardless of quality.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always inspect the final output; do not assume max iterations implies a passing result.

failure-mode.description: Router misclassification sends queries to the wrong expert with no automatic recovery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a confidence score to routing decisions and fall back to a generalist agent below threshold.

failure-mode.description: Concurrent writes to shared blackboard keys. Last write wins silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use immutable append semantics or explicit locking to prevent data loss.
